sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The magenta dot at (36, 17) moved to (35, 16)! It shifted up by 1 row and left by 1 column. The white bar also shrank from 50 to 48 pixels.
So clicking on a magenta dot makes it move diagonally up-left! This is similar to level 1 where the purple square moved along a diagonal path.
Let me track the magenta dots:
- id=9: was at (36, 17), now at (35, 16) - moved up-left
- id=10: at (36, 40) - unchanged
- id=11: at (55, 46) - unchanged
- id=12: at (56, 15) - unchanged
So the mechanic is:
1. Sky blue dots → click to turn magenta (2x2)
2. Magenta dots → click to move them diagonally up-left
The goal might be to move all magenta dots to specific positions, or to move them until they reach the blue shape or some target area.
Let me think about the pattern. The magenta dots seem to form two "arms" extending from the blue shape:
- Left arm: (35, 16), (56, 15)
- Right arm: (36, 40), (55, 46)
Wait, but the right arm dots would move up-left too, which would move them toward the center. Let me think about where each dot would end up if I keep clicking:
- (35, 16): clicking would move to (34, 15), then (33, 14), etc.
- (36, 40): clicking would move to (35, 39), then (34, 38), etc.
- (55, 46): clicking would move to (54, 45), then (53, 44), etc.
- (56, 15): clicking would move to (55, 14), then (54, 13), etc.
The blue shape is at rows 23-29, cols 31-37. The dots need to move toward it.
Actually, let me reconsider. In level 1, the purple square moved along a diagonal path. Maybe here, the magenta dots need to move along diagonal paths until they reach the blue shape or some target.
Let me try clicking on all magenta dots to see if they all move.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Magenta dots move diagonally up-left when clicked!
# Let me click on all 4 magenta dots to move them
magenta_dots = [(35, 16), (36, 40), (55, 46), (56, 15)]
moves = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in magenta_dots]
action(moves)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
